title: 7 Herbal Tinctures For Dizziness
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can help alleviate dizziness by addressing its underlying causes. Ginkgo biloba, for instance, is known for its ability to improve blood flow to the brain. This can be particularly beneficial for people experiencing dizziness due to reduced blood circulation. By taking a tincture made from Ginkgo biloba, you may find that your dizziness subsides, allowing you to go about your day with greater ease.  Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is another herb that can help alleviate dizziness. It has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce feelings of lightheadedness and disorientation. Taking a tincture made from Valeriana officinalis can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, making it easier to manage dizziness.  Zingiber officinale, or ginger, is another herb that can help alleviate dizziness. It has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of dizziness. Additionally, ginger has a calming effect on the stomach, which can help to reduce dizziness caused by digestive issues.  When taken as a tincture, these herbs can be absorbed quickly and easily into the body, providing fast relief from dizziness. This can be especially beneficial for people who experience frequent or severe dizziness, as it can help to improve their quality of life and reduce the impact of dizziness on their daily activities. By incorporating herbal tinctures into your daily routine, you may find that your dizziness becomes less frequent and less severe, allowing you to live life to the fullest.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures contains flavonoids and terpenoids as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to improve blood flow to the brain. The flavonoids, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, also act as antioxidants, reducing oxidative stress and protecting brain cells from damage. Terpenoids, like ginkgolides, help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which can be beneficial for people experiencing dizziness due to poor blood flow to the brain. By improving blood flow and protecting brain cells, ginkgo biloba tinctures may help to alleviate dizziness and improve overall brain function.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for dizziness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Ginkgo biloba leaves or 1/2 cup of dried leaves, and 2 cups of vodka (at least 80 proof).
Combine the Ginkgo biloba leaves and vodka in a clean glass jar. Make sure the leaves are completely covered by the vodka.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the tincture mix.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the Ginkgo biloba tincture in a cool, dark place and use 20-30 drops in water or tea 2-3 times a day to help with dizziness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tinctures contains a rich blend of bioactive constituents, including valerenic acid, valepotriates, and isovalerenic acid. These compounds have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce dizziness and balance problems. Valerenic acid, in particular, has been shown to interact with GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety that can contribute to dizziness. The valerian root's ability to calm the nervous system also helps to regulate blood pressure and reduce the symptoms of dizziness. By using Valeriana officinalis tinctures, individuals can experience a reduction in dizziness and improved balance due to its calming effects on the nervous system.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal tinctures for dizziness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Valeriana officinalis roots, 2 cups of vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried roots and vodka in the glass jar. Stir well to mix.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the tincture in the dark glass jar and take 10-20 drops, 2-3 times a day, as needed to help with dizziness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures contains the bioactive constituents gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, a common cause of dizziness. Gingerol and shogaol also have a stabilizing effect on the stomach, reducing nausea and vomiting that can contribute to dizziness. The spicy compounds in ginger tincture increase blood flow to the brain, which can help alleviate dizziness caused by poor circulation. The anti-inflammatory and circulatory properties of gingerol and shogaol work together to help alleviate dizziness and promote balance.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for dizziness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Zingiber officinale roots (about 10-12 roots). Wash and dry them.
Use a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to grind the roots into a fine powder.
Combine 1 cup of the powdered root with 2 cups of 95% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tinctures contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are bioactive constituents that contribute to its therapeutic effects. Flavonoids, such as vitexin and isovitexin, have a sedative action that helps to calm the nervous system and reduce dizziness. Alkaloids, including harmine and harmaline, have a spasmolytic effect, which can help to relax muscles and improve blood flow to the brain. Glycosides, like passiflorin, may help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, both of which can contribute to a reduction in dizziness. By addressing the underlying causes of dizziness, Passiflora incarnata tinctures may provide relief from this uncomfortable symptom.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal tinctures for dizziness
remedy_procedure: Gather 250g of fresh or dried Passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers.
Combine the plant material with 500ml of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and label it with the date and ingredients.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng tinctures contains bioactive compounds like ginsenosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds help to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the body. The ginsenosides, specifically Rg1 and Rb1, have a sedative effect on the nervous system and can help to stabilize blood pressure, reducing the likelihood of dizziness. Panax ginseng tinctures also contain antioxidants that can help to protect the body from oxidative stress, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness. By promoting a stable blood pressure and reducing inflammation, Panax ginseng tinctures can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness.
remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal tinctures for dizziness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Panax ginseng root, 2 cups of vodka (at least 40% ABV), and a glass jar with a lid.
Chop the dried Panax ginseng root into small pieces and place it in the glass jar.
Pour the vodka over the chopped ginseng root, making sure that the ginseng is completely covered.
Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tinctures contains a concentrated form of the active compounds present in oat extract. The main bioactive constituents of Avena sativa include avenanthramides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the inner ear and stabilize blood pressure. This can lead to a reduction in dizziness and vertigo symptoms. The flavonoids in Avena sativa tinctures, particularly avenanthramides, have been shown to improve blood flow to the brain and inner ear, further alleviating dizziness and other balance-related issues.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal tinctures for dizziness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Avena sativa roots, 2 cups of vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Chop the Avena sativa roots into small pieces and fill the glass jar 2/3 full.
Pour 2 cups of vodka over the chopped roots and make sure they are completely covered.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tinctures contains the bioactive constituents linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their calming and sedative properties. These constituents help to reduce anxiety and stress, common causes of dizziness. Linalool has a relaxing effect on the nervous system, which can help to stabilize blood pressure and improve balance. Linalyl acetate has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of vertigo and dizziness caused by inflammation in the inner ear. By reducing anxiety and inflammation, Lavandula angustifolia tinctures may help to alleviate dizziness and promote a sense of stability and calm.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal tinctures for dizziness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add the Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the jar and cover with vodka. Stir well and make sure flowers are fully covered.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the flowers.
Label the jar and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. Take 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, as needed to help alleviate dizziness.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea might help prevent dizziness in some cases. Certain herbs like ginger and peppermint have properties that can soothe the stomach and calm the nerves, which can help alleviate feelings of dizziness. If you experience dizziness, trying a gentle herbal tea might provide some relief.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a helpful way to manage dizziness, but drinking them daily isn't always safe. Some herbs, like peppermint and ginger, can be beneficial, but others, like valerian root and passionflower, can cause drowsiness or interact with medications. It's essential to be mindful of the herbs you're consuming and their potential effects.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with dizziness, but it's hard to predict exactly when you'll notice a difference. Some people feel better within a few hours, while others might not notice a change until after a few days of drinking the tea regularly. It really depends on the individual and the specific tea being used.
faq_time: If you're experiencing dizziness and want to try herbal tea, it's often best to drink it in the morning or early afternoon. At these times, the caffeine-free tea can help calm your stomach and alleviate dizziness without interfering with your evening sleep.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas can interact with prescription medications that cause dizziness. For example, valerian root tea can make dizziness worse, while ginger tea may help. The interactions depend on the tea and the medication. If you're taking prescription medications for dizziness and also drink herbal teas, be aware of the potential effects and adjust your tea consumption accordingly.
faq_pregnant: If you're pregnant and feeling dizzy, it's best to talk to your body and listen to its needs. Some herbal teas can help calm your stomach and reduce dizziness, but it's crucial to choose teas that are safe for pregnancy. Look for teas like ginger or peppermint, which are often recommended during pregnancy.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for dizziness can be a good option when breastfeeding, but it's essential to choose teas that are safe for both you and your baby. Some herbs, like peppermint and ginger, are commonly used and considered safe. However, other herbs like sage and pennyroyal can be toxic to babies, so it's crucial to check the ingredients.
title_2: Skiers, Stay on Track with 7 Herbal Tinctures for Dizziness
